channel.title: CyberNetSec - Policy and Compliance
channel.description: Latest policy and compliance threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: UK Cyber Survey: 43% of Businesses Breached, But Only 25% Have an IR Plan
item.description:
The UK government's latest Cyber Security Breaches Survey for 2025/2026 reveals a landscape of persistent risk, with 43% of businesses (an estimated 612,000) experiencing a breach in the past year. Phishing remains the dominant attack vector, implicated in 85% of successful breaches. Despite the hig...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Admits to Conspiring with BlackCat Gang
item.description:
Angelo Martino, a former ransomware negotiator, has pleaded guilty to conspiring with the notorious BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware gang. Martino abused his position at a crypto brokerage firm, using his insider knowledge of his clients' negotiating strategies and insurance limits to help BlackCat maxim...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Ransomware, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA and Partners Release Joint Guidance for Applying Zero Trust Principles to OT Environments
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), in partnership with the Department of War, DOE, FBI, and DOS, has published a comprehensive guide for implementing Zero Trust security principles within Operational Technology (OT) environments. The guidance provides a roadmap for sec...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Industrial Control Systems, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: Europol IOCTA Report: AI, Encryption, and Data Theft are Fueling an Industrialized Cybercrime Wave
item.description:
Europol's 2026 Internet Organised Crime Threat Assessment (IOCTA) report warns of an industrialized cybercrime landscape where AI, encryption, and proxies are making attacks faster and more sophisticated. The report highlights a significant shift in ransomware tactics towards data theft and extortio...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Policy and Compliance, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Europol Warns of AI-Powered 'Industrialized Cybercrime' in IOCTA 2026 Report
item.description:
Europol's 2026 Internet Organised Crime Threat Assessment (IOCTA) highlights a major shift towards the 'industrialization' of cybercrime, significantly accelerated by the use of Artificial Intelligence. The report states that AI is being used to increase the scale, speed, and efficiency of attacks. ...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: MITRE Warns of New Cyber Risks in AI and Cloud-Connected Medical Devices
item.description:
A new discussion paper from MITRE warns that the rapid integration of emerging technologies like AI, cloud computing, and post-quantum cryptography into medical devices is creating new and complex cybersecurity risks that could directly impact patient safety. The report, "Cybersecurity Risk Analysis...

Severity: Informational

Categories: IoT Security, Policy and Compliance, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Entertainment Industry Failing on Security Basics, MPA Report Reveals Widespread Risks
item.description:
The Motion Picture Association's (MPA) Trusted Partner Network (TPN) has released its first TPN STAR Report, revealing systemic security vulnerabilities across the global entertainment supply chain. The report finds that while most organizations have security policies, inconsistent execution of basi...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Policy and Compliance, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: Singapore Plans Major Cybersecurity Overhaul, Mandating PQC and Expanding CII Oversight
item.description:
Singapore is set to significantly strengthen its national cybersecurity posture with a series of regulatory updates announced by the government. The changes, to be rolled out over the next two years, will expand cybersecurity obligations to systems connected to Critical Information Infrastructure (C...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: State CISO Confidence Plummets as AI Threats Rise and Budgets Fall, Survey Finds
item.description:
Confidence among U.S. state chief information security officers (CISOs) has dropped dramatically, according to the 2026 NASCIO-Deloitte Cybersecurity Study. Only 26% of state CISOs feel 'extremely' or 'very' confident in their ability to protect state systems, a steep decline from 48% in 2022. The p...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: US Cracks Down on Southeast Asia Cyberscam Networks, Sanctions Cambodian Senator
item.description:
The U.S. government has launched a major, multi-agency crackdown on transnational cyberscam networks operating from Southeast Asia. The initiative, described as a 'new theater of war' against Chinese organized crime, involves sanctions, criminal charges, and asset seizures. The U.S. Treasury has san...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Phishing

Read Full Article →
